title: Tuhfat-ul-Muwahhidin / Gift to Monotheists (Book, Persian)
provisions: Raja Ram Mohan Roy
Context: Written to promote monotheism; part of Brahmo Samaj reform movement against idol worship and polytheism

title: Precepts of Jesus (Book, English)
provisions: Raja Ram Mohan Roy
Context: Extracted moral teachings of Jesus while rejecting miracles; reflected his belief in universal monotheism across religions

title: Upanishads - Translations (Book, Sanskrit to Bengali/English)
provisions: Raja Ram Mohan Roy
Context: Translated to revive original Vedantic philosophy of one God; foundation of Brahmo Samaj ideology

title: Sambad Kaumudi (Magazine, Bengali)
provisions: Raja Ram Mohan Roy
Context: Reformist magazine used to campaign against Sati Pratha and promote social reform in Bengal

title: Somprakash (Magazine, Bengali)
provisions: Ishwar Chandra Vidyasagar
Context: Reformist magazine promoting widow remarriage and women's education; linked to Hindu Widow Remarriage Act 1856

title: Ghulamgiri (Book, Marathi)
provisions: Jyotiba Phule
Context: Meaning 'Slavery'; written against caste oppression and upper-caste domination; part of Satya Sodhak Samaj movement (1873)

title: Sarvajanik Satyadharma (Book, Marathi)
provisions: Jyotiba Phule
Context: Advocated universal true religion; part of Satya Sodhak Samaj's agenda against untouchability and caste system

title: Veda Bhasya (Book, Sanskrit/Hindi)
provisions: Dayanand Saraswati
Context: Commentary on the Vedas; part of Arya Samaj's call to 'Go back to the Vedas' and reject Puranic literature

title: Veda Bhasya Bhumika (Book, Sanskrit/Hindi)
provisions: Dayanand Saraswati
Context: Introduction to Vedic commentary; supported Arya Samaj's rejection of priestly dominance and complex rituals

title: Satyarth Prakash (Book, Hindi)
provisions: Dayanand Saraswati
Context: Meaning 'The Light of Truth'; key ideological text of Arya Samaj; criticized idol worship, superstition and priestly dominance

title: A Theist's Confession of Faith (Book, English)
provisions: M.G. Ranade
Context: Written as leader of Prarthana Samaj; emphasized worship of God based on teachings of Maratha Bhakti saints

title: Asbab-e-Baghawat-e-Hind (Book, Urdu)
provisions: Sir Syed Ahmad Khan
Context: Rejected notion of Muslim conspiracy against British during 1857 revolt; part of Aligarh Movement to rebuild Muslim-British relations

title: Tahzib-ul-Akhlak (Journal, Urdu)
provisions: Sir Syed Ahmad Khan
Context: Reformist journal meaning 'Refinement of Morals'; promoted modern Western education for Muslims; opposed polygamy, purdah and Pir-Muridi; part of Aligarh Movement

title: Rast Goftar (Magazine, Gujarati)
provisions: Dadabhai Naoroji
Context: Meaning 'Truth Teller'; reformist magazine of Rahnumai Mazdayasna Sabha for removal of orthodox Parsi superstitions and women's upliftment

title: Indian Opinion (Newspaper, English)
provisions: Gandhi
Context: Published in South Africa to fight oppressive British measures against Indians; part of Gandhi's moderate phase (1894-1906) and later passive resistance

title: Kesari (Newspaper, Marathi)
provisions: Bal Gangadhar Tilak
Context: Provocative articles inspired Chapekar Brothers to assassinate W.C. Rand and Lt. Ayerst (1897); Tilak arrested for seditious writing; key organ of Extremist Phase

title: Maratha (Newspaper, English)
provisions: Bal Gangadhar Tilak
Context: English-language counterpart of Kesari; used to spread nationalist ideas during the Extremist Phase (1905-1915)

title: Indian Sociologist (Magazine, English)
provisions: Shyamji Krishna Varma
Context: Published from India House London; centre for nationalist ideas among Indian students abroad; financially supported by Madam Bhikaji Cama

title: Circular-e-Azadi (Newspaper, Urdu)
provisions: Ramnath Puri
Context: Published from Swadeshi Sevak Vancouver; part of revolutionary activities of Indians outside India

title: Free Hindustan (Newspaper, English)
provisions: Taraknath Das
Context: Published from United India House Seattle; part of revolutionary Indian nationalist network in USA

title: The Ghadar (Newspaper, Urdu/Punjabi)
provisions: Lala Har Dayal
Context: Published from Hindi Association of Portland USA; became the voice of the Ghadar Movement for armed struggle against British rule

title: Jugantar Patrika (Newspaper, Bengali)
provisions: Barindra Ghosh
Context: Revolutionary publication of Anushilan Samiti (founded 1902); acquired revolutionary character after Partition of Bengal (1905)

title: Sandhya Patrika (Newspaper, Bengali)
provisions: Brahmabandhav Upadhyay
Context: Revolutionary publication associated with Anushilan Samiti; promoted armed struggle against British rule in Bengal

title: Deshkar Kathna (Book, Marathi)
provisions: Sakharam Ganesh Devshankar
Context: Written in 1904; banned in 1910 for invoking nationalist sentiment during the Swadeshi Movement period

title: Why I Am an Atheist (Book, English)
provisions: Bhagat Singh
Context: Written in jail; Bhagat Singh was a member of HSRA founded by Chandrashekhar Azad; strongly influenced by Karl Marx; executed on 23 March 1931

title: Thakur Marjuli (Book, Bengali)
provisions: Dakshinaranjan Mitra
Context: Emotional appeal for the unity of Bengal during the Swadeshi Movement following Partition of Bengal (1905)

title: Amar Sonar Bangla (Song, Bengali)
provisions: Rabindranath Tagore
Context: Composed during Swadeshi Movement after Partition of Bengal (1905); later became national anthem of Bangladesh (1971)

title: Bharat Mata (Painting)
provisions: Abanindranath Tagore
Context: Painted during Swadeshi Movement; broke European tradition; depicted four hands holding bunch of paddy, piece of cloth, book and rudraksha mala; Oriental Society of Art founded

title: Wood's Dispatch 1854 (Dispatch, English)
provisions: Charles Wood
Context: Called Magna Carta of Education in India; rejected downward filtration theory; led to creation of Universities of Bombay, Madras and Calcutta

title: Nehru Report 1928 (Report, English)
provisions: Motilal Nehru
Context: Response to Lord Birkenhead's challenge to draft own constitution; demanded Dominion Status; opposed by Jawaharlal Nehru, Subhas Chandra Bose and S. Satyamurti who wanted Poorna Swaraj

title: Wardha Scheme of Education / Nai Talim 1937 (Education Scheme, Hindi)
provisions: Zakir Hussain
Context: Also called Gandhian Scheme; promoted universal basic education, ethics, morality and craft-based learning; implemented by Congress provincial governments

title: India for Indians (Slogan, English)
provisions: Dayanand Saraswati
Context: Call given as founder of Arya Samaj; viewed Hinduism as diluted by complex rituals and priestly dominance

title: Go back to the Vedas (Slogan, English)
provisions: Dayanand Saraswati
Context: Core mantra of Arya Samaj; rejected Puranic literature and advocated return to original Vedic teachings

title: Service to mankind is service to God (Slogan, English)
provisions: Ramakrishna Paramhansa
Context: Message given as priest at Kali Temple Dakshineshwar; inspired Swami Vivekananda to found Ramakrishna Mission (1897) for service to mankind

title: Yatra Jiva Tatra Shiva (Slogan, Sanskrit)
provisions: Ramakrishna Paramhansa
Context: Meaning 'Where there is life there is God'; justified idol worship; part of teachings that inspired Ramakrishna Mission

title: One caste, one God, one religion (Slogan, English/Malayalam)
provisions: Sree Narayana Guru
Context: Given as part of SNDP Movement (1901) in Kerala for upliftment of Ezhava untouchable community; later modified by disciples to 'No caste, no God, no religion'

title: Swaraj is my birthright and I shall have it (Slogan, English)
provisions: Bal Gangadhar Tilak (popularly associated)
Francis Baptist (originally coined)
Context: Associated with Extremist Phase (1905-1915); Tilak was a leader of Lal-Bal-Pal trio demanding assertive nationalism

title: Vande Mataram (Slogan, Sanskrit/Bengali)
provisions: Madam Bhikaji Cama (inscribed on first flag of India)
Context: Inscribed on yellow middle stripe of first Indian flag; unfurled at International Socialist Conference Stuttgart (1907); flag had green, yellow and saffron stripes

title: Do or Die (Slogan, English)
provisions: Gandhi
Context: Given during Quit India Movement at Gowalia Tank Bombay on 8 August 1942; called for non-violent mass struggle against British rule

title: Tum mujhe khoon do, main tumhe azadi doonga (Slogan, Hindi/Urdu)
provisions: Subhash Chandra Bose
Context: Given as leader of INA/Azad Hind Fauj (1943); formed Provisional Government of Free India; led Imphal-Kohima Campaign

title: Prabuddha Bharata / Awakened India (Journal, English)
provisions: Ramakrishna Mission
Context: English-language monthly journal focusing on spiritual, cultural, and philosophical discourse

title: Udbodhan (Journal, Bengali)
provisions: Swami Vivekananda
Context: Bengali monthly journal (started January 1899) to spread the teachings of Ramakrishna and the Vedanta movement

title: Vedanta Kesari (Magazine, English)
provisions: Ramakrishna Mission
Context: English monthly magazine (started 1914) dedicated to Vedanta philosophy and spiritual-cultural thought

title: Delhi Chalo (Slogan, Hindi/Urdu)
provisions: Subhash Chandra Bose
Context: Given as leader of the Indian National Army (Azad Hind Fauj) during World War II (1943–45); a call to march towards Delhi to overthrow British rule

title: Jai Hind (Slogan, Hindi)
provisions: Subhash Chandra Bose
Context: Popularised by Bose as the national greeting and battle cry of the Indian National Army (INA); later adopted as a patriotic slogan of independent India

title: Young India (Weekly Journal)
provisions: Mahatma Gandhi
Published: 1919–1931
Context: Used by Gandhi to spread his ideology of non-violence (Satyagraha) and resistance against British rule in India

title: New India (Newspaper, English)
provisions: Annie Besant
Context: Launched to promote reformist and nationalist views and support the Home Rule Movement; also associated with the publication Commonweal

title: The Indian Struggle (Book)
provisions: Subhas Chandra Bose
Context: Written to describe his role in the Indian independence movement and present his vision for India's freedom (covers the period 1920–1942)

title: National Herald (Newspaper)
provisions: Jawaharlal Nehru
Context: Founded in 1938 to express nationalist opinion and oppose British colonial rule in India
